NITI Aayog released a report on “Investment Friendliness Index”
articles.full_content:
Strengthening India’s Investment Ecosystem: States at the Core of India’s Growth Story Investment Friendliness Index to Benchmark, Enable and Accelerate State-level Reforms for attracting Investment Advancing the Vision of Viksit Bharat through Viksit Rajya@2047 and Stronger State Investment Ecosystems A Data-Driven Approach to Ease of Investment
During the 9th Governing Council Meeting of NITI Aayog held in July 2024, the Hon'ble Prime Minister tasked NITI Aayog with preparing an Investment-Friendly Charter comprising key policies, programmes, and processes required to attract investments. Subsequently, the Union Budget 2025–26 announced the development of an Investment Friendliness Index to strengthen the spirit of competitive and cooperative federalism by promoting reforms and fostering a conducive investment ecosystem across States.
NITI Aayog has released the report titled "Investment Friendliness Index (IFI)", which presents a structured and data-driven framework for assessing how effectively States and Union Territories create, enable, and sustain an environment conducive to investments. The Index evaluates key policy, institutional, regulatory, and infrastructure-related parameters that influence investment decisions and provides a comparative assessment of the investment ecosystem across States and Union Territories.
The report underscores that while national-level reforms provide the overarching policy direction for economic growth, State Governments play a pivotal role in shaping the investment climate through robust infrastructure, efficient regulatory frameworks, effective institutions, and predictable policy regimes. Strengthening these State-level investment ecosystems is essential for enhancing India's competitiveness, attracting greater domestic and foreign investment, and sustaining high economic growth. The Investment Friendliness Index seeks to foster competitive and cooperative federalism by encouraging States and Union Territories to adopt best practices and undertake continuous reforms in pursuit of the vision of Viksit Bharat @2047.
Why the Investment Friendliness Index Matters
India has witnessed sustained economic growth over the past three decades and is now on the path towards becoming a developed nation under the vision of Viksit Bharat @2047. Achieving this aspiration will require a significant increase in investment rates, with private investment expected to play a pivotal role in driving industrialisation, innovation, employment generation, and long-term economic growth.
While reforms undertaken by the Central Government establish the overarching policy and macroeconomic framework, investment decisions are ultimately influenced by the quality of the business environment at the State level. Factors such as availability of industrial land, quality of physical and digital infrastructure, regulatory efficiency, institutional capacity, and policy predictability are critical determinants of investment attractiveness. Rec
articles.summary: Strengthening India’s Investment Ecosystem: States at the Core of India’s Growth Story Investment Friendliness Index to Benchmark, Enable and Accelerate State-level Reforms for attracting Investment Advancing the Vision of Viksit Bharat through Viksit Rajya@2047 and Stronger State Investment Ecosystems A Data-Driven Approach to Ease of Investment During the 9th Governing Council Meeting of NITI Aayog held in July 2024, the Hon'ble Prime Minister tasked NITI Aayog with preparing an Investment-Friendly Charter comprising key policies, programmes, and processes required to attract investments. Subsequently, the Union Budget 2025–26 announced the development of an Investment Friendliness Index to strengthen the spirit of competitive and cooperative federalism by promoting reforms and fostering a conducive investment ecosystem across States. NITI Aayog has released the report titled "Investment Friendliness Index (IFI)", which presents a structured and data-driven framework for assessing how effectively States and Union Territories create, enable, and sustain an environment conducive to investments.

Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi lays foundation stone and dedicates to the nation various development projects worth over ₹4,700 crore in Chandigarh, spanning healthcare, education and road infrastructure
articles.full_content:
Chandigarh is witnessing a significant boost in healthcare, education and connectivity today; Delighted to launch projects that will benefit people here: PM Cleanliness is not a one-day activity;it is a way of life:PM When the COVID-19 pandemic struck, India was not a nation seeking help; it was a nation extending help to the world: PM In India, healthcare is no longer a privilege; it is becoming a right: PM
Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi today laid the foundation stone and dedicated to the nation various development projects worth over ₹4,700 crore in Chandigarh, spanning healthcare, education and road infrastructure. Highlighting the city's unique status as an exemplary model for systematic national progress, he emphasized its reputation for ensuring a better lifestyle and ease of living. Acknowledging the divine grace of Maa Chandi over the region, he reaffirmed the ruling administration's unwavering commitment to its continuous, structured growth. “The development of Chandigarh has always been the absolute priority of the current government,“ asserted Shri Modi.
Recalling the historic and massive overhaul of the nation's justice system implemented a year and a half ago, the Prime Minister pointed out the transition from punitive colonial laws to a justice-oriented framework. “The implementation of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita began from Chandigarh itself,“ observed Shri Modi.
Detailing the financial investments made to modernize the urban landscape, the Prime Minister listed transformative initiatives such as the Integrated Command and Control Center, Smart Traffic Management, and Digital Governance. Congratulating the residents on the inauguration of health, education, and infrastructure projects, he highlighted the massive capital infusion driving these futuristic upgrades. “More than two and a half thousand crore rupees have been spent on this mission to make the city high-tech,“ noted Shri Modi.
Charting his extensive daily itinerary dedicated to regional advancement, the Prime Minister mentioned his prior engagement in Jind, Haryana, and his upcoming visit to Jalandhar, Punjab. He framed his current stop as a vital geographic and administrative bridge seamlessly linking the neighboring states. “Our Chandigarh effectively connects both Haryana and Punjab,“ remarked Shri Modi.
Emphasizing the broader regional impact of localized urban growth, the Prime Minister explained how enhancements in the city directly elevate the quality of life for neighboring populations across Himachal Pradesh and Jammu and Kashmir. He specifically spotlighted the city's indispensable and central role in regional healthcare delivery. “For medical services, Chandigarh is a mega hub in this entire region,“ observed Shri Modi.
Announcing the major expansion of advanced healthcare facilities at the local medical institute, the Prime Minister introduced critical new additions including a Neuroscience Center, a Mother and Child Center, and a Critical Care Hospital Bl
articles.summary: Chandigarh is witnessing a significant boost in healthcare, education and connectivity today; Delighted to launch projects that will benefit people here: PM Cleanliness is not a one-day activity;it is a way of life:PM When the COVID-19 pandemic struck, India was not a nation seeking help; it was a nation extending help to the world: PM In India, healthcare is no longer a privilege; it is becoming a right: PM Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi today laid the foundation stone and dedicated to the nation various development projects worth over ₹4,700 crore in Chandigarh, spanning healthcare, education and road infrastructure. Highlighting the city's unique status as an exemplary model for systematic national progress, he emphasized its reputation for ensuring a better lifestyle and ease of living. Acknowledging the divine grace of Maa Chandi over the region, he reaffirmed the ruling administration's unwavering commitment to its continuous, structured growth.

61% Posts Vacant in Panchayati Raj Institutions, ₹3,243 Crore Shortfall in State Finance Commission Transfers: CAG
articles.full_content:
CAG Report Flags Lack of Quorum in Gram Sabhas, Inactive District Planning Committees and Serious Deficiencies in Financial and Administrative Management of Local Bodies
As many as 61 per cent of sanctioned posts in Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) in Chhattisgarh remain vacant, while local bodies received ₹3,243.46 crore less than the amount recommended by the State Finance Commission. In addition, most Gram Sabha meetings were held without the required quorum. These are among the significant findings highlighted in the audit of local bodies conducted by the Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG), which points to serious shortcomings in the financial management, administrative capacity and decentralized governance framework of local bodies in the State.
The Audit Report on Local Bodies of the Government of Chhattisgarh for the year ended March 2023, prepared by the Comptroller and Auditor General of India, was recently tabled in the Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly. The report examines the financial management, implementation of schemes, accounting systems, revenue administration and institutional capacity of Panchayati Raj Institutions and Urban Local Bodies, drawing attention to several systemic deficiencies and governance issues.
According to the report, although all 29 subjects listed in the Eleventh Schedule of the Constitution have been transferred to Panchayati Raj Institutions, the necessary operational framework for effective functional devolution has not been developed. The audit also observed that District Planning Committees did not hold meetings during the period 2018–2023, adversely affecting integrated planning and coordinated implementation of development programmes at the local level.
The audit further noted that the State Government did not transfer any funds to Panchayati Raj Institutions from designated revenue sources such as land revenue, land revenue cess and excise surcharge. During the period 2018–23, local bodies received ₹3,243.46 crore less than the amount recommended by the State Finance Commission, while only ₹398.20 crore was transferred from the heads of minor mineral royalty and stamp and registration fees.
The report also raises serious concerns over the functioning of Gram Sabhas. Audit scrutiny of 36 sample Gram Panchayats revealed that none of the Gram Sabha meetings met the prescribed quorum requirements. Despite this, no action was initiated against the officials responsible. The audit also found that several Gram Panchayats had failed to prepare their annual accounts in the prescribed format.
The audit highlights a critical shortage of manpower in Panchayati Raj Institutions. Out of 2,260 sanctioned posts in the Directorate, District Panchayats and Janpad Panchayats, only 872 posts were filled, leaving 1,388 posts vacant, representing a vacancy rate of 61 per cent. In addition, there was a shortage of 1,278 Panchayat Secretaries at the Gram Panchayat level, which has adversely affected administr
articles.summary: CAG Report Flags Lack of Quorum in Gram Sabhas, Inactive District Planning Committees and Serious Deficiencies in Financial and Administrative Management of Local Bodies As many as 61 per cent of sanctioned posts in Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) in Chhattisgarh remain vacant, while local bodies received ₹3,243.46 crore less than the amount recommended by the State Finance Commission. In addition, most Gram Sabha meetings were held without the required quorum. These are among the significant findings highlighted in the audit of local bodies conducted by the Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG), which points to serious shortcomings in the financial management, administrative capacity and decentralized governance framework of local bodies in the State.

Prime Minister shares glimpses from inauguration and laying of foundation stone for development projects spanning healthcare, education and road infrastructure in Chandigarh
articles.full_content:
Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi today shared glimpses from inauguration and laying of foundation stone for development projects in Chandigarh. Highlighting the city's unique status as an exemplary model for systematic national progress, Shri Modi emphasized its reputation for ensuring a better lifestyle and ease of living. Acknowledging the divine grace of Maa Chandi over the region, he reaffirmed the ruling administration's unwavering commitment to its continuous, structured growth.
Sharing glimpses in series of posts on X, Shri Modi said:
चिकित्सा सुविधाओं के साथ ही इंफ्रास्ट्रक्चर की बेहतरी को लेकर चंडीगढ़ के लोगों का जोश नए उत्साह से भर देने वाला है।
चिकित्सा सुविधाओं के साथ ही इंफ्रास्ट्रक्चर की बेहतरी को लेकर चंडीगढ़ के लोगों का जोश नए उत्साह से भर देने वाला है। pic.twitter.com/qUICJ4fW9r
चंडीगढ़ के लोगों ने स्वच्छता को लेकर एक नई अलख जगाई है। मैं यहां के अपने परिवारजनों को इसकी विशेष बधाई देता हूं। स्वच्छता के ऐसे अभियान आज देश के बहुत काम आ रहे हैं।
चंडीगढ़ के लोगों ने स्वच्छता को लेकर एक नई अलख जगाई है। मैं यहां के अपने परिवारजनों को इसकी विशेष बधाई देता हूं। स्वच्छता के ऐसे अभियान आज देश के बहुत काम आ रहे हैं। pic.twitter.com/Opa0KPzOXT
बीते 12 वर्षों में हमारी सरकार ने स्वास्थ्य सेवाओं के क्षेत्र में देश का सामर्थ्य बढ़ाया है, जिससे आज लोगों को इलाज की बेहतर सुविधाएं मिल रही हैं। हमारे प्रयासों का एक बड़ा परिणाम यह भी है कि भारत मेडिकल टूरिज्म का एक अहम डेस्टिनेशन बन रहा है।

बीमारियों के इलाज पर ही नहीं, Preventive Healthcare पर भी हमारा निरंतर फोकस रहा है। इसका बड़ा लाभ हमारी माताओं-बहनों और गरीब भाई-बहनों के साथ ही मिडिल क्लास को भी हो रहा है।
बीमारियों के इलाज पर ही नहीं, Preventive Healthcare पर भी हमारा निरंतर फोकस रहा है। इसका बड़ा लाभ हमारी माताओं-बहनों और गरीब भाई-बहनों के साथ ही मिडिल क्लास को भी हो रहा है। pic.twitter.com/SGPgFhvH7f

ICAR Technology Portfolio for Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) Conclave 2026
articles.full_content:
Over 75 Industries Join ICAR CSR Conclave 2026; Corporate Sector Commits to Scaling Agricultural Technologies through CSR and R&D ICAR Showcases CSR-Ready Technologies, Mobilizes Strategic Corporate Partnerships to Accelerate Agricultural Innovation and Farmers' Livelihoods
The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) and Agrinnovate India Limited (AgIn) jointly organized the “ICAR Technology Portfolio for Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) Conclave 2026” successfully at the NASC Complex, New Delhi. The conclave served as a premier platform to strengthen partnerships among ICAR, corporate organizations, industry leaders, philanthropic institutions, and other stakeholders to promote sustainable agricultural development through Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiatives.
The conclave showcased ICAR's portfolio of CSR-ready technologies, innovative research, and scalable interventions. The thematic focus areas of this year's conclave included Climate-Proofing of Indian Agriculture; Soil Health Improvement through Nature-Based Solutions and Crop Diversification; Agriculture for Health; Agricultural Skilling; And Women in Agriculture and Agricultural Extension. These priority areas are aimed at addressing critical agricultural challenges while promoting sustainable rural development through strategic CSR investments.
The event witnessed an overwhelming response from the corporate sector. Around 150 representatives from 75 industries participated in the conclave, reflecting the growing interest of industry in supporting agricultural transformation through CSR partnerships. In addition, 570 participants joined the programme online, while the live webcast on YouTube attracted more than 1,600 viewers, significantly expanding the outreach and engagement of the event across the country.
As a precursor to the conclave, four regional ICAR CSR & Technology Portfolio Roadshows were organized at Hyderabad (20 June 2026), Bengaluru (23 June 2026), Mumbai (1 July 2026), and Kolkata (7 July 2026). The roadshows were conducted to explore and mobilize CSR partners for strategic collaborations in sustainable agricultural development. These events brought together representatives from ICAR institutes, corporate organizations, industry leaders, startups, entrepreneurs, non-governmental organizations (NGOs), and the media to promote technology commercialization and strengthen CSR-driven partnerships. Collectively, the four roadshows witnessed participation from more than 500 stakeholders, with several organizations expressing strong interest in partnering with ICAR through CSR-supported initiatives.
The conclave concluded on a highly encouraging note, with leading industries and CSR organizations, namely Agriculture Insurance Company of India Limited (AICL), SBI Foundation, UCO Bank, ICICI Foundation, Biovet Pvt. Ltd., Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce & Industry (FICCI), INFAH, Venkateshwara Hatcheries Pvt. Ltd., Pidilite Industries Ltd., and Yes
articles.summary: Over 75 Industries Join ICAR CSR Conclave 2026; Corporate Sector Commits to Scaling Agricultural Technologies through CSR and R&D ICAR Showcases CSR-Ready Technologies, Mobilizes Strategic Corporate Partnerships to Accelerate Agricultural Innovation and Farmers' Livelihoods The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) and Agrinnovate India Limited (AgIn) jointly organized the “ICAR Technology Portfolio for Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) Conclave 2026” successfully at the NASC Complex, New Delhi. The conclave served as a premier platform to strengthen partnerships among ICAR, corporate organizations, industry leaders, philanthropic institutions, and other stakeholders to promote sustainable agricultural development through Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiatives. The conclave showcased ICAR's portfolio of CSR-ready technologies, innovative research, and scalable interventions.

articles.title: India's First Hydrogen Fuel Cell Train Set to Redefine Sustainable Rail Mobility with Capacity of Around 2,600 Passengers
articles.full_content:
The Smokeless Magic of Hydrogen Helps Build a Greener Indian Railways as It Powers the New-Era Train With Multi-layer Safety Systems Detecting Hydrogen Leaks, Heat, Flames and Smoke, the Train Is Certified as Safe
Indian Railways is set to flag off India's first Hydrogen Fuel Cell Trainset, a train that generates its own electricity onboard using hydrogen, the cleanest fuel known. It produces near-zero emissions at the point of use. This milestone marks the latest chapter in the evolution of how Indian Railways has powered its trains, reflecting India's broader journey from coal and steam to cleaner, more sustainable sources of energy.
Over the past 12 years, rapid electrification has significantly reduced dependence on imported diesel, paving the way for the next leap in clean rail mobility. Today, with over 99% of Broad Gauge routes electrified, Indian Railways is taking that journey a step further. Unlike conventional electric trains that draw power from overhead lines, the Hydrogen Fuel Cell Trainset generates electricity onboard through a chemical reaction between hydrogen and oxygen, with water vapour as its only by-product.
In a sense, the train once again carries its own source of power, as steam and diesel locomotives once did. But instead of burning traditional fuels such as coal or diesel, hydrogen generates electricity inside the train using oxygen from the atmosphere, eliminating combustion and dependence on an external power supply. As electricity is generated onboard through clean hydrogen technology, the train represents the greenest form of rail propulsion, powering the future of sustainable mobility. To complement this advanced propulsion system, India has equipped the train with multi-layer safety systems capable of detecting hydrogen leaks, heat, flames and smoke. With an operational speed of 75 kmph on the Jind–Sonipat section and a design speed of 110 kmph, the train is not only safer but also faster on this 89 km route.
Most hydrogen passenger trains currently operating globally comprise only two or three coaches and are primarily deployed on short regional routes. In contrast, the Indian Railways trainset has been configured as a 10-coach passenger train with a capacity of around 2,600 passengers, demonstrating the scalability of hydrogen-powered rail transport for high-capacity passenger operations.
But hydrogen is famously flammable, and that naturally raises a question in everyone's mind: is it safe to put thousands of passengers on a train running on a gas that can catch fire so easily? Here is a simple explanation of how the train works, and everything Indian Railways has done to make it safe.
How Does a Hydrogen Train Actually Work?
Unlike conventional diesel locomotives that burn fuel to generate mechanical power, a hydrogen train carries a small power plant onboard in the form of a Proton Exchange Membrane (PEM) fuel cell. Hydrogen stored in the train's cylinders combines with oxygen from the surrounding air inside
articles.summary: The Smokeless Magic of Hydrogen Helps Build a Greener Indian Railways as It Powers the New-Era Train With Multi-layer Safety Systems Detecting Hydrogen Leaks, Heat, Flames and Smoke, the Train Is Certified as Safe Indian Railways is set to flag off India's first Hydrogen Fuel Cell Trainset, a train that generates its own electricity onboard using hydrogen, the cleanest fuel known. It produces near-zero emissions at the point of use. This milestone marks the latest chapter in the evolution of how Indian Railways has powered its trains, reflecting India's broader journey from coal and steam to cleaner, more sustainable sources of energy.

CSIR-Central Road Research Institute (CSIR-CRRI) today commenced its year-long Platinum Jubilee Celebrations with the launch of a nationwide first-phase implementation of advanced indigenous road infrastructure technologies across 16 towns or locations across the country.
articles.full_content:
Union Minister Dr. Jitendra Singh calls for wider industry partnerships to take indigenous road technologies from laboratories to highways Dr. Jitendra Singh Launches MSS+ Smart Mix Pro Plant, Calls for Stronger Industry Partnerships to Scale Up Indigenous Road Technologies CSIR-CRRI Showcases Next-Generation Road Technologies, Strengthens Industry Partnerships for Wider Deployment
The CSIR-Central Road Research Institute (CSIR-CRRI) today commenced its year-long Platinum Jubilee Celebrations (July 16, 2026 – July 16, 2027) with the launch of a nationwide first-phase implementation of advanced indigenous road infrastructure technologies across 16 towns or locations in the country. The initiative marks a significant step towards demonstrating and scaling up innovative, sustainable road construction and maintenance technologies developed by CSIR-CRRI through strategic collaborations with industry and implementing agencies.
The celebrations were inaugurated by Union Minister of State (Independent Charge) for Science & Technology and Earth Sciences; Minister of State in the Prime Minister's Office, Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions, Department of Atomic Energy and Department of Space, Dr. Jitendra Singh, who called for stronger collaboration between scientific institutions, industry and State governments to ensure that home-grown technologies are implemented on a much larger scale across the country.
Before inaugurating the celebrations, Dr. Jitendra Singh visited the technology exhibition and interacted with scientists, researchers and industry representatives. He reviewed a range of indigenous technologies developed by CSIR-CRRI in road engineering, pavement technologies, intelligent transportation systems, road safety and sustainable construction materials. The Minister also launched the Automatic Modified Mix Seal Surfacing (MSS+) Smart Mix Pro Mixing Plant, designed for the large-scale implementation of CSIR-CRRI's indigenous Modified Mix Seal Surfacing (MSS+) technology. He inaugurated the AI-enabled Network Survey Vehicle (NSV) for comprehensive road infrastructure assessment and dedicated the PATCH-FILL pothole repair technology, reinforcing the Institute's efforts towards smarter, safer and environmentally sustainable road infrastructure.
The programme was attended by Dr. (Mrs.) N. Kalaiselvi, Director General, CSIR & Secretary, DSIR; Dr. Ch. Ravi Sekhar, Director, CSIR-CRRI; former Directors of the Institute; senior scientists, academicians, industry leaders, policymakers, engineers, representatives of Central and State Government organisations and technology partners.
Addressing the gathering, Dr. Jitendra Singh said that India's unprecedented infrastructure expansion under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi has created a significant opportunity for scientific institutions to contribute cutting-edge technologies for roads, bridges and transportation systems. Referring to the country's expanding network of highways,
articles.summary: Jitendra Singh calls for wider industry partnerships to take indigenous road technologies from laboratories to highways Dr. Jitendra Singh Launches MSS+ Smart Mix Pro Plant, Calls for Stronger Industry Partnerships to Scale Up Indigenous Road Technologies CSIR-CRRI Showcases Next-Generation Road Technologies, Strengthens Industry Partnerships for Wider Deployment The CSIR-Central Road Research Institute (CSIR-CRRI) today commenced its year-long Platinum Jubilee Celebrations (July 16, 2026 – July 16, 2027) with the launch of a nationwide first-phase implementation of advanced indigenous road infrastructure technologies across 16 towns or locations in the country. The initiative marks a significant step towards demonstrating and scaling up innovative, sustainable road construction and maintenance technologies developed by CSIR-CRRI through strategic collaborations with industry and implementing agencies.

A review meeting was held under the chairmanship of Shri Ashish Thaker, Additional Director General, Department of Telecommunications (DoT), Gujarat LSA, with officials of Gujarat Fibre Grid Network Limited (GFGNL), the Aravalli District Administration, and BSNL
articles.full_content:
A review meeting was held under the chairmanship of Shri Ashish Thaker, Additional Director General, Department of Telecommunications (DoT), Gujarat LSA, with officials of Gujarat Fibre Grid Network Limited (GFGNL), the Aravalli District Administration, and BSNL to review the progress of the 4G Saturation Project and the BharatNet Project, flagship initiatives of the Ministry of Communications, Government of India, aimed at strengthening digital connectivity across rural and remote areas of Aravalli district, Gujarat.
The district administration and the Forest Department assured full cooperation and committed to expediting the allotment of land required for the remaining 4G Saturation Project sites to facilitate timely completion of the project.
During the meeting, the progress of both the 4G Saturation Project and the BharatNet Project was reviewed in detail. It was informed by Dr. K B Sharma Director,DoT that a total of 24, 4G Saturation sites have been planned in Aravalli district, and the district administration assured that land allotment for the remaining 7 sites would be completed at the earliest.
On the occasion, Shri Ashish Thaker, Addl. DGT, along with Ms. K. Krupali Mistri, Dy. DDO, felicitated Shri Jagdish bhai Patel, Sarpanch of Akarund Gram Panchayat, Dhansura Block, Aravalli District, for securing the Second Rank among Gram Panchayats in Gujarat based on telecom performance metrics. The recognition highlights the Gram Panchayat's exemplary contribution towards the promotion of digital connectivity and telecom infrastructure.
Subsequently, Shri Ashish Thaker inspected the 4G Saturation Project site at Vanaj village along with officials from DoT, BSNL, and GFGNL. The site, developed using indigenous telecom equipment under the Government of India's initiative to promote indigenous technology, was reviewed in detail. During the inspection, he issued directions for further improvements to ensure robust and reliable mobile connectivity for the local population.
The Department of Telecommunications reiterated its commitment to working closely with the State Government, district administration, GFGNL, and Telecom Service Providers to ensure timely completion of telecom infrastructure projects and to provide seamless digital connectivity to every village in Gujarat.
articles.summary: A review meeting was held under the chairmanship of Shri Ashish Thaker, Additional Director General, Department of Telecommunications (DoT), Gujarat LSA, with officials of Gujarat Fibre Grid Network Limited (GFGNL), the Aravalli District Administration, and BSNL to review the progress of the 4G Saturation Project and the BharatNet Project, flagship initiatives of the Ministry of Communications, Government of India, aimed at strengthening digital connectivity across rural and remote areas of Aravalli district, Gujarat. The district administration and the Forest Department assured full cooperation and committed to expediting the allotment of land required for the remaining 4G Saturation Project sites to facilitate timely completion of the project. During the meeting, the progress of both the 4G Saturation Project and the BharatNet Project was reviewed in detail.
